known_evidence.extracted_text: Interferometers detect minute variations in laser phase shifts, but are highly susceptible to seismic noise.

constraints: Must account for seismic noise floor below 10Hz.
Must be technologically feasible within the 2015 timeframe.
candidate_experiments: Upgrade interferometers with active seismic isolation and higher-power lasers to detect binary mergers.
Abandon ground-based interferometry and immediately lobby for a space-based constellation (e.g. LISA).
Focus entirely on resonant mass detectors (Weber bars) at ultra-low cryogenic temperatures.
